title: The Forest That Remembered
description: The Corrupted Forest, Eldertree Village, the Tempo's chase, and the Arbiter's duel.

lines: The Corrupted Forest wore its sickness openly. Trees bent at angles wood shouldn't permit.
Light came from the wrong direction — not from the sky but from somewhere beneath the roots.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: We're being tracked. Since the bridge. Don't reach for the sword.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Someone is studying us. They've had four opportunities to ambush us and passed on all of them. That's not predation. That's professional curiosity.

steps.text: A calling card fell from a branch above: a small leather token stamped with the image of a running hound.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The Tempo. They're not attacking us. They're playing.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: How delightful. We have a nemesis.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Those are the same thing to a good story.

lines: Eldertree Village huddled at the forest's edge. Its sacred trees were dying from the inside out. Sap ran black. Leaves fell upward.
Deep in the village, they found a man who had fled the capital.

lines.speaker: Refugee
lines.text: Chancellor Mordaine. She doesn't rule the kingdom. She IS the kingdom now.

lines.speaker: Refugee
lines.text: She believes she's helping. That's the worst part. She has a word for it. Says everything has to be... calibrated.

steps.text: He used that word — calibrated — and none of them thought anything of it. Why would they? It was just a word.
